title: Build Contact page with form
description:
Create Contact page with spam-resistant form.

## Form Fields
- Name (required)
- Email (required)
- Company/Business name
- Phone (optional)
- What brings you here? (dropdown)
  - Website development
  - Custom software
  - System integration
  - SaaS audit
  - General inquiry
- Brief description (textarea)
- Honeypot field (hidden, for spam detection)

## Additional Content
- Direct email address
- Response time expectation
- Link to booking for immediate scheduling

## Form Behavior
- Client-side validation
- Honeypot spam filtering
- Success message after submission
- Error handling

## Note
Backend (Lambda + SES) is separate task in Phase 3.
This task creates the frontend form only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Form renders correctly
- [ ] Client-side validation works
- [ ] Honeypot field hidden
- [ ] Mobile-friendly form
- [ ] Accessible (labels, focus states)

title: Document content update workflow
description:
Create documentation for updating site content.

## Documentation Needed

### How to Update Text
- Which files to edit (src/pages/*.astro)
- How to preview locally (npm run dev)
- How to deploy (git push)

### How to Add Blog Posts
- Create new .md file in src/content/blog/
- Frontmatter format
- Image handling
- Preview and publish

### How to Update Services/Pricing
- Location of content
- What to update
- Verification steps

### Emergency Procedures
- How to rollback a bad deploy
- Who to contact

## Format
- README.md in repo root or docs/
- Step-by-step with examples
- Assumes basic git/terminal familiarity

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Documentation covers common updates
- [ ] Steps are clear and accurate
- [ ] Tested by following the docs

title: Build Services page with all offerings
description:
Build Services page with all service offerings.

## Services to Feature

### Custom Software & Automation
Practical AI and automation solutions customized for your business:
- Automatic transcription, storage, and triage of voicemails
- Social media review monitoring
- Custom integrations between your tools

### Website Development
Modern websites without monthly SaaS fees:
- You own the code
- No platform lock-in
- Fast, SEO-optimized
- Starting at $500-2,000

### System Integration
Stop being the middleware:
- Connect tools that don't talk to each other
- Eliminate manual data entry
- Automate workflows

### SaaS Audit & Optimization
$200 flat fee audit:
- Full review of software subscriptions
- Recommendations for consolidation
- Typical savings: 30-50% on monthly spend

### Hourly Tech Consulting
Direct access when you need it:
- No hosting commitment required
- Strategic technology advice
- Problem-solving sessions

## Design
- Service cards with icons or illustrations
- 'Get Started' or 'Learn More' CTAs
- Link to Pricing page for details

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All services listed with descriptions
- [ ] Clear CTAs for each service
- [ ] Responsive grid/list layout
- [ ] Brand styling applied

title: Implement contact form backend (Lambda + SES)
description:
Build serverless backend for contact form submissions.

## Architecture
```
Contact Form → API Gateway → Lambda → SES → Your Email
                                  ↓
                            DynamoDB (optional, for tracking)
```

## Lambda Function
- Validate input (required fields, email format)
- Check honeypot field (reject if filled)
- Rate limiting (optional)
- Send email via SES
- Return success/error response

## SES Setup
- Verify sender domain (mainstreetlogic.com)
- Or verify single email address for testing
- Create email template

## Email Content
- From: noreply@mainstreetlogic.com
- To: your email
- Subject: "New Contact Form Submission"
- Body: All form fields formatted nicely

## Infrastructure
- Add Lambda + API Gateway to CloudFormation
- Or deploy separately with SAM/Serverless

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lambda function deployed
- [ ] API Gateway endpoint created
- [ ] SES sending emails
- [ ] Honeypot rejecting bots
- [ ] Error handling works
- [ ] CORS configured for site domain

title: Write blog post: Stop Being the Middleware
description:
Write blog post highlighting integration value.

## Title
"Stop Being the Middleware: When Your Tools Don't Talk"

## Outline
1. **Hook**: You shouldn't be the one copying data between systems
2. **The Problem**:
   - Manual data entry between CRM and email
   - Exporting from one tool to import to another
   - Spreadsheets as the "glue"
3. **The Cost**:
   - Time spent (calculate hours/week)
   - Errors introduced
   - Delayed information
   - Employee frustration
4. **Why It Happens**:
   - SaaS tools want lock-in
   - "Integrations" are often basic
   - Enterprise solutions cost too much
5. **The Solution**:
   - Custom integrations
   - Automation tools (Zapier, Make, or custom)
   - API connections
6. **Real Examples**:
   - CRM → Email → Calendar sync
   - Invoice → Accounting auto-entry
   - Form → CRM → Welcome email
7. **CTA**: Link to integration services

## Target
~1000-1500 words

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Relatable examples
- [ ] Quantified time savings
- [ ] CTA to integration service

title: Implement client authentication (magic links)
description:
Set up passwordless authentication for client portal.

## Architecture
```
Client requests login → Lambda generates magic link → SES sends email
Client clicks link → Lambda validates token → Sets session cookie
```

## Components
- Login page with email input
- Lambda function: generate and validate tokens
- Token storage (DynamoDB with TTL)
- Session management (JWT in httpOnly cookie)

## Flow
1. Client enters email on login page
2. Lambda generates unique token, stores in DynamoDB (15 min TTL)
3. SES sends email with magic link
4. Client clicks link → token validated → session created
5. Session cookie set for subsequent requests

## Security
- Tokens expire in 15 minutes
- Single-use tokens
- httpOnly, secure, sameSite cookies
- HTTPS only

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Login page accepts email
- [ ] Magic link email sent
- [ ] Link logs user in
- [ ] Session persists across requests
- [ ] Logout works

title: Set up dark mode CSS infrastructure
description:
Set up Tailwind CSS dark mode support and define dark color palette.

## Tasks
1. Configure Tailwind for class-based dark mode (`darkMode: 'class'`)
2. Add dark mode color variants to global.css @theme
3. Update body styles with dark mode variants
4. Update link styles for dark mode

## Dark Mode Colors
| Element | Light Mode | Dark Mode |
|---------|------------|-----------|
| Background | light-bg (#F5F5F5) | dark-bg (#1A2530) |
| Text | navy (#2D4966) | soft-gray (#D1CDC7) |
| Links | steel-blue (#7B9DBB) | steel-blue (same) |
| Cards/Sections | white | navy or darker |

## Implementation Notes
- Use `dark:` prefix for dark mode styles
- Add `.dark` class to `<html>` element to activate
- Ensure smooth transition between modes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tailwind dark mode configured
- [ ] Dark color palette defined
- [ ] Base body/link styles have dark variants
- [ ] Adding `dark` class to html switches colors

title: Write blog post: 5 Signs Ready for Custom Software
description:
Write blog post highlighting custom software value.

## Title
"5 Signs Your Business Is Ready for Custom Software"

## Outline
1. **Intro**: Custom software isn't just for big companies
2. **Sign 1: You're Outgrowing Your Tools**
   - Features you need don't exist
   - Workarounds are getting complex
3. **Sign 2: You're Paying for Features You Don't Use**
   - Enterprise pricing for SMB needs
   - Forced into higher tiers
4. **Sign 3: Your Process Is Unique**
   - Off-the-shelf doesn't fit
   - You've customized it beyond recognition
5. **Sign 4: Integration Hell**
   - Tools don't connect
   - You need custom bridges anyway
6. **Sign 5: Competitive Advantage**
   - Your process IS the product
   - Can't get edge from commodity software
7. **What Custom Software Actually Costs**:
   - Not as much as you think
   - Compare to years of SaaS
   - You own it forever
8. **CTA**: Link to custom software service

## Target
~1200-1800 words

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each sign has concrete example
- [ ] Addresses cost concerns
- [ ] CTA to consultation

title: Create theme toggle component
description:
Create a toggle button component for switching between light and dark modes.

## Requirements
- Sun/moon icons (or similar) to indicate current mode
- Click to toggle between modes
- Accessible (keyboard navigation, aria labels)
- Smooth icon transition

## Behavior
1. On click: toggle `dark` class on `<html>` element
2. Save preference to localStorage (`theme: 'light' | 'dark' | 'system'`)
3. On page load: 
   - Check localStorage for saved preference
   - If none, check `prefers-color-scheme` media query
   - Apply appropriate class before paint (prevent flash)

## Script Requirements
- Must run before page renders to prevent flash of wrong theme
- Should be inline in `<head>` or loaded with high priority
- Handle SSR (no window/document access at build time)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Toggle component renders in header
- [ ] Clicking toggles dark class on html
- [ ] Preference saved to localStorage
- [ ] System preference respected on first visit
- [ ] No flash of wrong theme on page load
- [ ] Keyboard accessible (Enter/Space to toggle)
- [ ] Screen reader announces state

## New Logo Assets

- `logo-and-name-dark.png` - Use in light mode (dark logo on light background)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Toggle visible in header on all pages
- [ ] Theme persists across page navigation
- [ ] Theme persists across browser sessions
- [ ] Respects prefers-color-scheme on first visit
- [ ] Logo swaps appropriately for each mode
- [ ] All pages render correctly in both modes
- [ ] No flash of wrong theme on page load

title: Configure CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions
description:
Set up automated build and deployment pipeline.

## Workflows Needed

### ci.yml (main workflow)
- Trigger: PR and push to main
- Jobs:
  - Build & test
  - Deploy infrastructure (on main only)
  - Deploy to staging (on PR)
  - Deploy to production (on main)
  - Comment PR with staging URL

### cleanup.yml
- Clean up old staging previews
- Remove artifacts older than X days

## Reference
Use thehousefinch.com/.github/workflows/ as template.

## Secrets Required
- AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
- AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
- AWS_REGION

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Builds run on every PR
- [ ] Staging preview deployed per PR
- [ ] PR comment with preview URL
- [ ] Production deploys on merge to main
- [ ] CloudFront cache invalidated after deploy
close_reason: CI/CD pipeline fully configured in .github/workflows/ci.yml. Includes: build/test, CloudFormation validation, staging deploys for PRs, production deploys on main, Lighthouse CI, E2E tests with Playwright, dependency health checks.

title: Write blog post: Why Your $29/Month Website Costs More
description:

## Title
"Why Your $29/Month Website Is Actually Costing You More"

## Outline
1. **Hook**: That "affordable" website isn't as cheap as you think
2. **The Hidden Costs**:
   - Subscription adds up ($348/year forever)
   - Premium features you need cost extra
   - Limited customization → workarounds
   - Slow changes → lost opportunities
3. **The Comparison**:

4. **What You Don't Own**:

   - Export limitations
   - Platform changes affect you

   - $5-15/month hosting
   - You own the code

~1000-1500 words

- Dark mode: `/images/logo/logo-and-name-light.png` (light logo on dark bg)

| Header bg | bg-white | bg-dark-bg or bg-navy |
| Nav links | text-navy | text-soft-gray |

title: ADR-004: Booking Integration
description:
Document booking decision.

## Decision
**Calendly** (free tier) with Google Calendar sync.

## Context
Need consultation booking that syncs to calendar without manual work.

## Options Considered
1. Calendly free tier (chosen)
2. Cal.com (self-hosted)
3. Cal.com (cloud)
4. Acuity
5. Custom booking system

## Rationale
- Free tier sufficient (1 event type)
- Unlimited bookings
- Built-in Google Calendar sync
- Professional user experience
- Zero development effort

## Trade-offs
- Limited to 1 event type on free
- Calendly branding
- Third-party dependency

## Migration Path
Can move to Cal.com self-hosted later if needed.

## Status
**Accepted**

| Text | text-soft-gray | text-soft-gray (same) |
| Links | text-steel-blue | text-steel-blue |
| Border/divider | border-medium-steel | border-dark-steel |

## Notes
- Footer is already dark-ish (navy background)
- May need subtle adjustment for dark mode
- Ensure sufficient contrast in both modes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Footer readable in both modes
- [ ] Links visible and accessible
- [ ] Consistent with overall dark theme

title: Write blog post: The $500/Month SaaS Trap
description:
Write blog post highlighting SaaS audit value.

## Title
"The $500/Month SaaS Trap (And How to Escape It)"

## Outline
1. **Hook**: Most small businesses don't realize how much they spend on software
2. **The Creep**: How $10 here and $30 there adds up
3. **Common Offenders**: 
   - Paying for seats you don't use
   - Enterprise features you don't need
   - Overlapping tools doing same job
4. **The Math**: Example breakdown of typical small business
5. **Escape Plan**:
   - Audit your subscriptions
   - Identify redundancy
   - Negotiate or switch
   - Consider one-time alternatives
6. **CTA**: Link to SaaS Audit service or checklist download

## Target
~1000-1500 words

## SEO
- Target: "small business SaaS costs", "reduce software subscriptions"
- Include specific dollar examples
- Actionable takeaways

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Post written and edited
- [ ] SEO meta tags configured
- [ ] Internal links to services
- [ ] CTA included

title: ADR-001: Hosting Infrastructure
description:
Document hosting decision.

## Decision
**S3 + CloudFront** for static site hosting.

## Context
Need cost-effective, reliable hosting that demonstrates our own capabilities.

## Options Considered
1. S3 + CloudFront (chosen)
2. Vercel
3. Netlify
4. GitHub Pages

## Rationale
- Costs pennies per month
- Global CDN included
- Already using for thehousefinch.com
- Demonstrates AWS capability to clients
- No vendor lock-in

## Consequences
- Need CloudFormation/IaC skills
- No built-in CI/CD (use GitHub Actions)
- More setup than Vercel/Netlify

## Status
**Accepted**

title: Build site health dashboard
description:
Dashboard showing client's site performance metrics.

## Metrics to Show
- Lighthouse scores (Performance, SEO, Accessibility, Best Practices)
- Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS)
- Uptime percentage
- Last deployment date

## Data Collection
- Scheduled Lighthouse runs (Lambda + Lighthouse CI)
- Store results in DynamoDB or S3
- Uptime via CloudWatch or external monitor

## UI
- Score cards with trend indicators
- Historical chart (last 30 days)
- Alerts for issues
- "What these mean" explanations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lighthouse scores displayed
- [ ] Historical data shown
- [ ] Uptime visible
- [ ] Actionable if scores drop

title: Configure DNS and domain cutover
description:
Point mainstreetlogic.com to AWS infrastructure.

## Pre-Cutover Checklist
- [ ] All pages working on staging
- [ ] Forms submitting correctly
- [ ] SSL certificate ready (ACM or CloudFront default)
- [ ] CloudFront configured with domain
- [ ] All CTAs tested

## Domain Info
- Domain: mainstreetlogic.com
- Registrar: Namecheap
- Current DNS: Route 53 (account 205074708100)

## Cutover Steps
1. Document current DNS records
2. Request ACM certificate for mainstreetlogic.com
3. Validate certificate (DNS validation in Route 53)
4. Update CloudFront with alternate domain name
5. Update Route 53 A/AAAA records to point to CloudFront
6. Wait for propagation
7. Test site on production domain
8. Verify HTTPS working

## Rollback Plan
Keep CloudFront distribution URL handy for quick redirect back if needed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DNS updated
- [ ] Site accessible at mainstreetlogic.com
- [ ] HTTPS working
- [ ] All pages loading
- [ ] Forms working on production

title: Integrate consultation booking (Calendly)
description:
Set up consultation booking that syncs to Google Calendar.

## Decision: Calendly Free Tier
- 1 event type (Free Consultation)
- Unlimited bookings
- Google Calendar sync built-in
- Embed widget or link to hosted page

## Setup Steps
1. Create Calendly account
2. Connect Google Calendar
3. Create "Free Consultation" event type
   - Duration: 30 minutes
   - Availability: Set your hours
   - Buffer time between meetings
4. Get embed code or booking link
5. Add to Contact page and CTAs

## Integration Options
- **Embed widget**: Inline on Contact page
- **Popup widget**: Button triggers modal
- **Link**: Button opens Calendly page

## Recommendation
Start with link to Calendly page (simplest). Can embed later if desired.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Calendly account created
- [ ] Google Calendar connected
- [ ] Event type configured
- [ ] Booking link on Contact page
- [ ] CTA buttons link to booking
- [ ] Test booking creates calendar event

title: ADR-005: Analytics
description:
Document analytics decision.

## Decision
**CloudFlare Web Analytics** (free, cookie-free).

## Context
Need analytics without cookie consent banners (brand value: respecting users).

## Options Considered
1. CloudFlare Web Analytics (chosen)
2. Plausible ($9/mo)
3. Umami (self-hosted)
4. Google Analytics 4
5. No analytics

## Rationale
- Free
- No cookies required
- No consent banner needed
- Privacy-respecting
- Simple dashboard
- Aligns with brand values

## Trade-offs
- Less detailed than GA4
- No goal/conversion tracking
- Limited historical data

## Migration Path
Can add Plausible later if need more features.

## Status
**Accepted**

description:
Create content and optimize for search engines.

## Definition of Done

title: Copy and integrate brand assets
description:
Copy brand assets from planning repo and integrate into website.

## Source
~/Development/mainstreetlogic/brand/

title: Initialize Astro project with Tailwind CSS
description:
Set up a fresh Astro project replacing the existing Vite/React site.

## Cleanup (keep these)
- .git/
- .github/
- .beads/
- infrastructure/
- AGENTS.md
- .gitattributes
- .gitignore (will need updates)

## Remove
- src/ (old React code)
- public/
- package.json, package-lock.json
- vite.config.js
- index.html
- node_modules/

## Astro Setup
1. npm create astro@latest . -- --template minimal --typescript strict
2. npx astro add tailwind
3. Configure astro.config.mjs for static output

## Tailwind Configuration
Custom design tokens from brand/colors/palette.md:
```js
colors: {
  'steel-blue': '#7B9DBB',
  'navy': '#2D4966',
  'medium-steel': '#5C7E9E',
  'dark-steel': '#3C5E7E',
  'soft-gray': '#D1CDC7',
  'light-bg': '#F5F5F5',
  'dark-bg': '#1A2530',
}
```

## Typography
- Font: Inter (from Google Fonts or Fontsource)
- Headings: semibold/bold
- Body: regular weight

## Project Structure
```
src/
  layouts/
    BaseLayout.astro
  components/
    Header.astro
    Footer.astro
  pages/
    index.astro
    about.astro
    services.astro
    pricing.astro
    contact.astro
    blog/
      index.astro
  styles/
    global.css
public/
  favicon.svg
  images/
    logo/
    hero/
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Astro dev server runs (npm run dev)
- [ ] Tailwind styles working with brand colors
- [ ] TypeScript strict mode enabled
- [ ] Static build succeeds (npm run build)
- [ ] Basic placeholder pages render

title: Create email-gated hosting discount offer
description:
Create lead magnet with email capture for hosting discount.

## Offer
"50% off your first 2 months of hosting"
- Requires email to receive coupon code
- Code emailed immediately after signup

## Implementation

### Landing Section
- On Pricing page or dedicated section
- Headline: "Try it risk-free"
- Form: Email only
- Submit → Email with coupon code

### Email Delivery
- Use newsletter provider (Buttondown) or
- Lambda + SES for immediate delivery

### Coupon Code
- Generate unique codes, or
- Single code with usage tracking

### Email Content

- Body: Code, how to redeem, CTA to schedule consultation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Signup form captures email
- [ ] Email delivered with coupon
- [ ] Code is trackable
- [ ] Terms are clear
- [ ] Adds to newsletter list (optional)

title: ADR-002: Frontend Framework
description:
Document framework decision.

## Decision
**Astro** with Tailwind CSS.

## Context
Need a modern framework for a static marketing site.

## Options Considered
1. Astro (chosen)
2. Next.js (static export)
3. React + Vite (existing)
4. 11ty
5. Plain HTML

## Rationale
- Ships zero JavaScript by default
- Excellent Lighthouse scores
- Great for content sites
- Tailwind integration built-in
- Same stack as thehousefinch.com

## Consequences
- Newer ecosystem
- Learning curve for Astro specifics
- Limited dynamic features (by design)

## Status
**Accepted**

Build out the essential pages with compelling content.

title: Set up monitoring and alerting
description:
Implement monitoring to catch issues before users report them.

## Monitoring Scope
- Uptime monitoring
- Performance monitoring
- Error tracking

## Options

title: Main Street Logic Website Launch
description:
Build mainstreetlogic.com - a modern website for a technology consultant serving small businesses.

## Business Context
Main Street Logic helps small businesses escape technology friction through:
- Custom software & automation
- Website development (no monthly SaaS fees)
- System integration
- SaaS audit & optimization

## Target Audience
Small business owners (5-50 employees) struggling with:
- Integration friction (tools don't talk)
- Subscription creep ($500+/month in SaaS)
- One-size-fits-none software
- Slow changes (weeks to update a website)

## Key Positioning
**Developer, Not Agency** - Direct access to a developer who builds custom solutions.

## Success Metrics
- Sub-3s page load on mobile
- Clear path to consultation booking
- Lead capture working
- SEO-optimized for local searches
- Mobile-first responsive design

## Phases
1. Foundation: Astro setup, infrastructure, brand assets, CI/CD
2. Core Pages: Homepage, Services, About, Contact, Pricing, Blog
3. Integrations: Contact form, booking, newsletter, analytics, lead magnets
4. Content & SEO: Blog posts, optimization, social sharing
5. Client Portal: Future phase for client dashboard
6. Launch: DNS cutover, monitoring, documentation

title: Performance optimization and Core Web Vitals
description:
Optimize site performance for excellent user experience.

## Targets
- Largest Contentful Paint (LCP): <2.5s
- First Input Delay (FID): <100ms
- Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS): <0.1
- Lighthouse Performance: >90

## Optimization Checklist

### Images
- [ ] Convert to WebP/AVIF where supported
- [ ] Responsive images with srcset
- [ ] Lazy loading for below-fold
- [ ] Explicit width/height (prevent CLS)

### CSS/JS
- [ ] Astro ships zero JS by default ✓
- [ ] Purge unused CSS (Tailwind does this)
- [ ] Inline critical CSS if needed
- [ ] No render-blocking resources

### Fonts
- [ ] Subset fonts to needed characters
- [ ] font-display: swap
- [ ] Preload critical fonts
- [ ] Consider system fonts

### Caching
- [ ] CloudFront caching configured
- [ ] Cache-Control headers set
- [ ] Static assets have hashes

## Testing
- Lighthouse (Chrome DevTools)
- PageSpeed Insights
- WebPageTest

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All Core Web Vitals green
- [ ] Lighthouse Performance >90
- [ ] Page load <3s on 3G
- [ ] No layout shifts visible

title: Build Blog structure and layout
description:
Create blog infrastructure for content marketing.

## Structure
- /blog - Blog index page (list of posts)
- /blog/[slug] - Individual post pages

## Components

### BlogIndex.astro
- List of posts with:
  - Title
  - Date
  - Excerpt
  - Read more link
- Pagination (if needed later)

### BlogPost.astro (layout)
- Post title
- Date and reading time
- Author (optional)
- Content area (Markdown)
- Related posts (optional)
- CTA at bottom

### Post Frontmatter
```yaml
title: "Post Title"
date: 2026-01-25
excerpt: "Brief description"
tags: ["saas", "automation"]
```

## Content Location
- src/content/blog/*.md

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Blog index lists posts
- [ ] Individual posts render
- [ ] Markdown content works
- [ ] Posts sorted by date
- [ ] Mobile responsive
- [ ] SEO meta tags per post

title: Accessibility audit (WCAG 2.1 AA)
description:
Ensure site meets accessibility standards.

## WCAG 2.1 AA Checklist

### Perceivable
- [ ] Alt text on all images
- [ ] Color contrast ≥4.5:1 for text
- [ ] Information not conveyed by color alone
- [ ] Text resizable to 200%

### Operable
- [ ] All functionality via keyboard
- [ ] Visible focus indicators
- [ ] No keyboard traps
- [ ] Skip navigation link

### Understandable
- [ ] Language attribute on <html>
- [ ] Form labels associated with inputs
- [ ] Error messages clear and specific
- [ ] Consistent navigation

### Robust
- [ ] Valid HTML
- [ ] ARIA used correctly (if at all)
- [ ] Works with screen readers

## Testing Tools
- axe DevTools extension
- WAVE evaluator
- Lighthouse accessibility
- Keyboard-only navigation test
- VoiceOver/NVDA screen reader test

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lighthouse Accessibility >90
- [ ] No critical axe violations
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Screen reader test passed

title: Build Pricing page with transparent model
description:
Build Pricing page with transparent pricing model.

## Pricing Structure (from docs/pricing.md)

### Website Hosting & Maintenance
Monthly Plans:
- Starter: $12/mo - Hosting + 3 hrs maintenance (simple marketing sites)
- Professional: $15/mo - Hosting + 3 hrs maintenance (complex sites)

Included maintenance:
- Security updates and patches
- Minor content updates
- Bug fixes
- Performance monitoring
- SSL certificate management
- Daily backups

Additional work: $30/hour beyond included hours

### Project Work
- Website Development: Starting at $500-2,000
- Custom Software & Automation: Quoted per project
- SaaS Audit: $200 flat

### The Backlog Model
Unlike traditional agencies:
- Your backlog is yours - see everything done, in progress, upcoming
- Prioritize what matters to your business
- Add requests anytime
- No waiting for project managers

### FAQ Section
- Month-to-month, cancel anytime
- Hours don't roll over
- Maintenance vs custom work distinction
- Transparent billing

## Design
- Clean pricing cards/table
- FAQ accordion or list
- CTA to Contact/Get Started

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All pricing tiers displayed
- [ ] Project work pricing shown
- [ ] FAQ section present
- [ ] Clear CTAs
- [ ] Mobile responsive

title: Set up newsletter signup and delivery
description:
Implement newsletter subscription system.

## Options Evaluation

### Option A: Buttondown ($9/mo)
- Simple, developer-friendly
- Markdown email composition
- Good deliverability
- Easy API

### Option B: SES + Custom ($0.10/1000)
- Cheapest at scale
- Full control
- Need to build: subscriber management, unsubscribe, templates
- More work

## Recommendation
**Buttondown** for simplicity. Migrate to custom SES later if volume justifies.

## Implementation
1. Create Buttondown account
2. Configure signup form embed
3. Add to footer (all pages)
4. Add to homepage (optional dedicated section)
5. Set up welcome email
6. Plan first newsletter content

## Signup Form
- Email field only (keep it simple)
- Success message: "Thanks! Check your email to confirm."
- Double opt-in (GDPR compliant)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Signup form in footer
- [ ] Subscribers added to list
- [ ] Confirmation email sent
- [ ] Unsubscribe works
- [ ] Welcome email configured

title: Phase 5: Client Portal (Future)
description:
Build a client portal for ongoing client engagement.

## Scope
- Client authentication (magic links)
- Backlog view (see their tasks, request new ones)
- Site health dashboard (Lighthouse scores, uptime)
- Analytics snapshot (traffic trends)
- Billing history (past invoices, current plan)
- Support requests (submit issues, track status)
- AI-generated improvement suggestions

## Definition of Done
- Clients can log in via magic link
- Dashboard shows site health metrics
- Backlog visible and requestable
- Invoice history accessible
- Support ticket flow working

## Notes
This is a future phase. Launch site without portal first.
Revisit when 5+ active clients.

title: ADR-003: Contact Form Backend
description:
Document contact form decision.

## Decision
**Lambda + SES** with honeypot spam protection.

## Context
Need spam-resistant contact form that's free/cheap and self-hosted.

## Options Considered
1. Lambda + SES (chosen)
2. Formspree
3. Netlify Forms
4. Google Forms
5. mailto link only

## Rationale
- No recurring cost (AWS free tier)
- Full control over data
- Honeypot is user-friendly (no CAPTCHA)
- Demonstrates serverless capability
- Can add features later (DynamoDB storage, etc.)

## Consequences
- More setup than SaaS options
- Need to handle SES verification
- Need to build/maintain Lambda code

## Status
**Accepted**

title: Build analytics snapshot for clients
description:
Simple analytics view for clients.

## Metrics
- Page views (last 30 days)
- Unique visitors
- Top pages
- Traffic sources
- Trend vs. previous period

## Data Source
- If using CloudFlare: API access
- If using Plausible: Shared link or API
- Could aggregate from multiple sources

## UI
- Simple charts (line graph for traffic)
- Top 5 pages list
- Source breakdown
- Period selector (7d, 30d, 90d)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Traffic metrics visible
- [ ] Top pages shown
- [ ] Trends displayed
- [ ] Data updates regularly

title: Phase 1: Foundation & Infrastructure
description:
Set up the technical foundation for the website.

title: Configure SEO metadata and structured data
description:
Implement SEO best practices across the site.

## Technical SEO

### Meta Tags (per page)
- title (unique, <60 chars)
- description (unique, <160 chars)
- canonical URL
- robots (index, follow)

### Open Graph
- og:title
- og:description
- og:image
- og:url
- og:type

### Twitter Cards
- twitter:card
- twitter:title
- twitter:description
- twitter:image

### Structured Data (JSON-LD)
- Organization schema
- LocalBusiness schema (if applicable)
- Service schema for services page
- Article schema for blog posts

### Technical
- XML sitemap (auto-generated)
- robots.txt
- Canonical URLs
- Clean URL structure

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All pages have unique title/description
- [ ] OG tags working (test with social debuggers)
- [ ] Structured data validates (Google Rich Results Test)
- [ ] Sitemap generated and accessible
- [ ] robots.txt configured

title: Submit site to search engines
description:
Submit site for search engine indexing.

## Google Search Console
1. Verify site ownership (DNS or HTML file)
2. Submit sitemap.xml
3. Request indexing for key pages
4. Monitor for crawl errors

## Bing Webmaster Tools
1. Verify ownership
2. Submit sitemap
3. Monitor indexing

## Initial SEO Check
- Verify robots.txt allows crawling
- Verify sitemap.xml is generated
- Check meta robots tags
- Verify canonical URLs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Google Search Console verified
- [ ] Sitemap submitted to Google
- [ ] Bing verified (optional)
- [ ] No crawl errors
- [ ] Key pages indexed within 1-2 weeks

title: Build About page with story and approach
description:
Build the About page with company story and approach.

## Content Sections

### Hero
'Empowering small businesses with intelligent automation is our mission at Main Street Logic.'

### Our History
Founded in 2025, Main Street Logic was created by seasoned technologists with deep experience in software engineering, artificial intelligence, and cloud-native architectures.

Before launching Main Street Logic, our team spent years modernizing Fortune 100 financial service organizations—bringing complex, distributed systems into the cloud, building microservice platforms, and developing AI-driven solutions at scale.

Now, we bring that expertise to Main Street—making modern technology accessible, effective, and personal for the businesses that power our communities.

### Locations We Serve
Main Street Logic is capable of providing solutions globally, but focuses on creating exceptional customer experiences in Richmond, Virginia and the surrounding area. We are available for in-person consultation if desired.

We run solutions for some clients directly in the cloud, taking advantage of superior scalability and reliability while building practical AI and automation solutions with cutting edge cloud provider solutions.

### What We Do
At Main Street Logic, we work directly with small business owners to uncover high-impact opportunities for automation. Our mission is to deliver practical, intelligent software solutions that simplify operations, reduce manual overhead, and unlock growth – without the complexity of enterprise systems.

We enable practical usage of AI solutions customized for your business such as automatic transcription, storage, and triage of voicemails, monitoring of social media reviews, website facelifts, Search Engine Optimization, and more.

## Design
- Clean, spacious layout matching brand mockups
- Section dividers with subtle wave backgrounds
- Responsive for mobile/desktop

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All content sections rendered
- [ ] Responsive layout
- [ ] Brand styling applied
- [ ] SEO meta tags configured
